channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. launches operation to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military announced the launch of an operation to end Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and reopen the waterway to global shipping traffic.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. military announced the launch of an operation to end Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and reopen the waterway to global shipping traffic.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Middle East analyst on the latest Persian Gulf attacks
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Aaron David Miller, a senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, about the latest attacks in the Persian Gulf.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Aaron David Miller, a senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, about the latest attacks in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Canadian prime minister says Europe will anchor world order
item.description: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney tells European leaders they, not the U.S., will anchor the world order in the future.
item.content:encoded: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney tells European leaders they, not the U.S., will anchor the world order in the future.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Manoush Zomorodi talks about her new book, 'Body Electric'
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Manoush Zomorodi, host of NPR's TED Radio hour and author of the new book, "Body Electric."
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Manoush Zomorodi, host of NPR's TED Radio hour and author of the new book, "Body Electric."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. starts operation to reopen Strait of Hormuz for commercial ships, Tuesday's election in Indiana puts Trump's political power to the test, SCOTUS delays restrictions on abortion pill mifepristone.
item.content:encoded: U.S. starts operation to reopen Strait of Hormuz for commercial ships, Tuesday's election in Indiana puts Trump's political power to the test, SCOTUS delays restrictions on abortion pill mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Ohio holding primary elections for several key races
item.description: Ohio is holding primary elections for key races, including governor, U.S. Senate and the House. Many voters say they are concerned about the rising cost of living.
item.content:encoded: Ohio is holding primary elections for key races, including governor, U.S. Senate and the House. Many voters say they are concerned about the rising cost of living.
item.dc:creator: Abbey Marshall

item.title: Detained and deported: A Missouri man must make a new life in the Netherlands
item.description: A Missouri resident detained by immigration authorities was deported this year to the Netherlands, a country he has not lived in since he was 5 years old. Now he's learning a new language and trying to make a new life.
item.content:encoded: A Missouri resident detained by immigration authorities was deported this year to the Netherlands, a country he has not lived in since he was 5 years old. Now he's learning a new language and trying to make a new life.
item.dc:creator: Addison Zanger

item.title: Florida's drought conditions leave alligators with no place to go
item.description: Drought conditions in Florida are so bad that some lakes are drying up and alligators are running out of room.
item.content:encoded: Drought conditions in Florida are so bad that some lakes are drying up and alligators are running out of room.
item.dc:creator: Regan McCarthy

item.title: Trump approves permit for 'Keystone Light'
item.description: President Trump has approved a border crossing permit for a Canadian crude oil pipeline many are calling "Keystone Light."
item.content:encoded: President Trump has approved a border crossing permit for a Canadian crude oil pipeline many are calling "Keystone Light."
item.dc:creator: Kayla Desroches

item.title: Tuesday's election in Indiana puts Trump's influence to the test
item.description: Tuesday will be a test of President Trump's political power as voters in Indiana decide whether to back state senate incumbents Trump wants out or the challengers he has endorsed.
item.content:encoded: Tuesday will be a test of President Trump's political power as voters in Indiana decide whether to back state senate incumbents Trump wants out or the challengers he has endorsed.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
